bullets.text: Myanmar's former leader Aung San Suu Kyi, a Nobel Peace Prize laureate, has been moved to house arrest after being detained since the 2021 military coup.
Oil prices have reached their highest point since 2022 following reports that former President Trump will be briefed on potential new military options regarding Iran.

Fears of a resurgence in Somali piracy have emerged after three vessels were hijacked in a single week, with pirates seemingly taking advantage of international naval forces being diverted to the Middle East.
A former Canberra vascular surgeon will face disciplinary action for lying under oath and attempting to interfere with a witness during criminal proceedings for an alleged rape.
Violence erupted in Alice Springs following the arrest of a man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl, with dozens gathering outside the hospital where he was treated.
The UK has elevated its terrorism threat level to severe after two Jewish men were stabbed in London, while Australia and South Korea have agreed to strengthen energy supply cooperation.
US stocks reached record highs, marking Wall Street's best month since 2020, as investors anticipate significant AI spending to drive growth, overshadowing concerns about the Middle East conflict.
Israel deployed a laser system to the UAE to counter Iranian missiles, representing a notable instance of defense cooperation between the two nations.
Air Canada suspended its full-year 2026 guidance due to uncertainties surrounding rising jet fuel prices.
